title: United Airlines could see 'substantial' EPS growth over coming quarters, analysts say

title: United Airlines (UAL) Reports Q1 Loss, Tops Revenue Estimates

title: United Airlines' cargo sales slump 37% in Q1
text: The higher they climb, the harder they fall. United Airlines is the king of cargo among the U.S. international passenger airlines and took its cargo business to unparalleled heights during the COVID crisis, when supply chains were shattered and companies needed alternative transport.

title: United Airlines sees sunnier skies ahead after 1Q results meet expectations
text: United Airlines reported its 1Q earnings on Tuesday after the bell, in line with expectations after warning investors that higher jet fuel prices, capacity growth and lower demand in January and February would impact its bottom line. The firm reported an adjusted net loss of $207 million, or $0.63 per share, versus the $0.73 per share expected by the Street.

title: United Airlines international bookings increase by 15% over year-ago, stock climbs in premarket
text: United Airlines Holdings Inc. UAL, -1.20% said Thursday that international bookings are up 15% in March from year-ago. The carrier said it has expanded its international flying by 25% from 2022 to meet increased demand for travel outside of the U.S. “The airline is already the largest carrier across both the Atlantic and Pacific and this summer's schedule includes nearly 25 new routes,” the carrier said.
